title: Portal hypertension clinic documentation
source_review_basis: full authoritative documents inspected and archived; item-level retain/rewrite/remove/add comparisons recorded

assessment.text: Supports recording complication risk, clinician-entered MELD result, ultrasound, AFP, endoscopy, varices, and specialist status.
assessment.rationale: The exact cirrhosis monitoring section supports established portal-hypertension context, varices, ultrasound, AFP, endoscopy, clinician-entered score, bleeding risk, and specialist status without calculating risk.

source.title: Cirrhosis in over 16s: assessment and management — Recommendations
source.jurisdiction: England and Wales; international evidence requiring UAE pathway adaptation
source.population: People aged over 16 with suspected or established cirrhosis and its complications.
source.setting: Primary and specialist care as qualified by the cited recommendations.
source.exclusions: Supports clinician-entered cirrhosis, ascites, portal-hypertension, complication, monitoring, endoscopy, ultrasound, and result-review documentation without calculating scores or generating treatment.

exact_location.heading: Recommendations 1.2.1–1.2.8 — monitoring complications, HCC, and varices

source.evidence_paraphrase: Supports recording complication risk, clinician-entered MELD result, ultrasound, AFP, endoscopy, varices, and specialist status.

assessment.text: Supports documenting established ascites, infection-risk context, bleeding, refractory status, and clinician-decided plan without generating antibiotics or procedures.
assessment.rationale: The exact complication section supports documenting established ascites and clinician-entered complication review and follow-up without generating medicine, procedure, or admission.
